articles.title: Stafanie Taylor's late blitz saves West Indies from Scotland scare at Women's T20 World Cup
articles.description: West Indies Women secured a tense seven-run victory over Scotland Women in the ICC Women's T20 World Cup. Stafanie Taylor's explosive 47* guided West Indies to 153/6, a total Scotland almost chased down thanks to Darcey Carter's 59. Crucial late wickets from Aaliyah Alleyne and Hayley Matthews sealed the win.

articles.title: Kawasaki Ninja 1100 SX Gets Free Pannier Kit Worth Rs 1.25 Lakh
articles.description: Kawasaki is offering the Ninja 1100 SX with a complimentary hard pannier kit valued at Rs 1.25 lakh. This offer is valid until June 30, 2026, and is available on a first-come, first-served basis while stocks last.

articles.title: Rajnath writes to Gadkari, seeks elevated corridor over Shaheed Path in Lucknow
articles.description: Special attention should also be paid in the design to provide for traffic movement via a “clover leaf” at the Kamta intersection near the high court, as this spot constantly faces heavy traffic jams, the letter added.

articles.title: India's Net Direct Tax Collections Climb To Rs 5.21 Lakh Crore Till June 17
articles.description: India net direct tax collections for early FY27 rose 14.64 percent to Rs 5.21 lakh crore, led by strong corporate taxes, advance tax and STT, signaling robust economic activity

articles.title: Tamannaah Bhatia’s Lavish Lokhandwala Home Is All About Family Warmth, Luxury Interiors And Ocean Views

articles.description: AIIMS will release the BSc Nursing Admit Card 2026 on June 19. Candidates can download their hall tickets from aiimsexams.ac.in using their Registration ID, email ID and password. Check steps to download, details to verify and exam day instructions

articles.title: India weather update: IMD alerts for heavy rain in south India as dust storms, heatwave grip north
articles.description: IMD issued dual weather alerts: Heavy rains to lash Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Assam, while heatwaves and dust storms hit Delhi-NCR, Punjab, and UP.

articles.title: “Insane amount of muscle”: Las Vegas Raiders RB's massive offseason transformation ignites social media buzz
articles.description: Las Vegas Raiders running back Ashton Jeanty has become a major talking point after a viral offseason workout photo showcased his noticeably bigger physique. NFL fans flooded social media with reactions, with some comparing him to Derrick Henry while others questioned the hype. The buzz arrives as new offensive coordinator Klint Kubiak prepares to build the Raiders' offence around Jeanty and Brock Bowers. With expectations rising ahead of training camp, the former Boise State star's dramatic transformation has only added to the excitement surrounding Las Vegas as it enters the 2026 NFL season.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can...” why purpose, not circumstances, often decides who keeps going when life becomes unexpectedly hard — learn the uplifting wisdom from the philosopher who challenged the world to think beyond comfort and hardship
articles.description: Quote of the day by Friedrich Nietzsche: “He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.” We often wonder why some people break under pressure while others survive the hardest trials imaginable. The answer lies in a profound human truth about resilience and inner purpose. When you possess a powerful why to live for, your mind can endure any difficult how.

articles.title: Who are Alexander Zverev’s parents? Meet Irina and Alexander Sr., the former tennis players behind his success
articles.description: Alexander Zverev’s French Open victory in 2026 was also a proud moment for his parents, Irina and Alexander Zverev Sr. Both were former professional tennis players who later became coaches. After moving from the Soviet Union to Germany, they helped raise and train two professional tennis players, Mischa and Alexander. Their influence remains a major part of Alexander’s success story.

articles.title: More than just referees: Penso, Nesbitt and Mayo take center stage to make World Cup history
articles.description: In a groundbreaking moment for sports, the 2026 FIFA World Cup witnessed history as Tori Penso led an all-American female officiating crew during a men's match. Together with Kathryn Nesbitt and Brooke Mayo, they became the second all-female team and the first American trio to step onto such a prestigious stage, a significant stride towards promoting gender equality in football.

articles.title: 'COVID vaccine has worst side effects ever': Dr Rogers' explosive claim rocks fiery Senate hearing
articles.description: A congressional hearing titled 'How the Corruption of Science Has Impacted Public Perception and Policies Regarding Vaccines' turned into a heated debate over an unpublished study comparing chronic illnesses in vaccinated versus unvaccinated children.

articles.title: Dwyane Wade's wife Gabrielle Union reveals why she stayed with him after learning about his child with another woman
articles.description: Dwyane Wade and his wife, popular actress Gabrielle Union, have always been vocal about the issues in their relationship even though they have been together for more than a decade now. While the two have been married for over a decade now, both Gabrielle Union and Dwyane Wade have spoken about how their marriage is far from perfect.

articles.title: Who are Scottie Scheffler’s children? Everything to know about World No.1 golfer’s sons Bennett and Remy
articles.description: Scottie Scheffler and his wife, Meredith Scudder, are parents to two sons, Bennett and Remy. Bennett was born in May 2024 and has already shared several special moments from his father’s golf career. Remy arrived in March 2026 and visited Augusta National just nine days later. Despite his success in golf, Scheffler says being a great father matters most to him.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Who won yesterday's matches & moved up the table? Check all results and the latest group standings
articles.description: FIFA World Cup 2026 Yesterday's Match: England kicked off their World Cup 2026 campaign with a commanding 4-2 victory over Croatia. Ghana secured a dramatic late win against Panama, while Colombia triumphed 3-1 against debutants Uzbekistan. Czechia and South Africa played out a hard-fought 1-1 draw, with England, Ghana, and Colombia emerging as the day's biggest winners.

articles.title: Adah Sharma to make her Marathi cinema debut with ‘Gajra’; See first look
articles.description: Marathi cinema: Adah Sharma has announced her Marathi cinema debut with Gajra, a thriller inspired by true events. The first-look poster shows her as a blood-splattered bride. Directed by Shreyas Jadhav, the film is set for a 2027 release.

articles.title: “I believe that was intentional”: Matt Wallace fuels Charlie Kirk lookalike speculation with viral UFC Freedom 250 clip
articles.description: A brief UFC Freedom 250 video shared by Matt Wallace triggered online speculation after viewers spotted a man some believed looked like Charlie Kirk. Wallace later claimed it was a lookalike but offered no evidence. The debate came shortly after Erika Kirk remembered her late husband and shared how excited he had been about the possibility of a UFC event at the White House.

articles.title: Facing Indus Waters Treaty Fallout, Bilawal Bhutto Threatens India In Pakistan Parliament: 'We'll Retaliate'
articles.description: Bhutto warned India over water supplies, while India dismissed the remarks as an attempt to divert attention from Pakistan's internal water crisis and terror concerns.

articles.title: 'TVK a democratic revolution’: After 55 years in CPI, veteran leader Mahendran joins Vijay’s party
articles.description: Veteran CPI leader C Mahendran has joined actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, citing a desire to strengthen communist ideology and public welfare. Mahendran, a seasoned politician with a five-decade record, believes TVK offers a platform for ordinary citizens and aims to eradicate corruption. He envisions the party as a mass movement, marking a significant shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: Who is Abraham Hermosillo Alvarez? Mexican national accused of leading White House UFC Freedom 250 terror plot
articles.description: DHS says the alleged ringleader overstayed a visa, later received DACA protection and now faces federal terrorism-related charges.

articles.title: Pakistan PM's visit to Switzerland postponed due to electronic signing of US-Iran deal: Spokesman
articles.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has postponed a visit to Switzerland for a US-on Thursday. "The proposed visit has been postponed as the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding has already been electronically signed, has entered into force, and is now under implementation," spokesman Mosharraf Zaidi said, adding Pakistan would support the next phase of several "technical-level" tracks. bur-ceg/lga

articles.title: Normalise conversations to rid stigma against sickle cell disease: Doctors
articles.description: Social stigma around sickle cell disease in India fuels secrecy, delayed treatment and complications; doctors urge awareness, counselling and early screening.

articles.title: Lokayukta police conduct surprise inspection at Udupi CMC over pending applications
articles.description: Lokayukta police conducted a surprise inspection at Udupi CMC, addressing public complaints about pending applications and administrative delays.

articles.title: Saif Ali Khan honours his father Tiger Pataudi at Winchester College plaque unveiling ceremony
articles.description: Saif Ali Khan attended a special ceremony at Winchester College to honour his father, cricket legend Tiger Pataudi, during the unveiling of a commemorative plaque celebrating his legacy.

articles.title: PM-Kisan 23rd instalment on June 20: Rs 18,880 crore to be transferred to 9.44 crore farmers
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will release the 23rd instalment of the PM-Kisan scheme on June 20 in West Bengal, transferring Rs 18,880 crore to over 9.44 crore farmers. He will also launch crop insurance schemes, an Agri-Tech platform, and the National Natural Farming Mission, alongside inaugurating rural road projects.

articles.title: Why US senators are threatening to cut Pete Hegseth’s travel budget
articles.description: Lawmakers from both parties say the Pentagon has failed to provide key information about a deadly Iran strike and controversial military operations at sea, prompting an unusual push to restrict the defence secretary's spending.

articles.title: UP TGT result expected to be released soon at upessc.up.gov.in: Check steps to download scorecards
articles.description: The Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission (UPESSC) is expected to announce the UP TGT Result 2026 soon on its official website. The result will determine the selection status of thousands of candidates who appeared for the recruitment examination. Along with the scorecards, the commission will release the merit list and category-wise cut-off marks.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Portugal coach defends playing Cristiano Ronaldo for full 90 minutes in draw with DR Congo
articles.description: Portugal coach Roberto Martinez defends keeping Cristiano Ronaldo on the field during a frustrating World Cup draw against DR Congo.

articles.title: PM Narendra Modi joins Ranveer Singh’s Dhurandhar trend as film’s craze reaches new heights
articles.description: Ranveer Singh’s Dhurandhar craze reached a new high after BJP’s official handle shared a PM Narendra Modi video inspired by the film, highlighting its theatrical and streaming success worldwide today.

articles.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old monument in Wiltshire linked to early Stonehenge traditions
articles.description: A remarkable 5,000-year-old wooden monument, predating Stonehenge by 500 years, has been unearthed near Bulford, Wiltshire. This discovery, featuring two large posts aligned with the midsummer sunrise and midwinter sunset, suggests sophisticated ancient astronomy and ceremonial traditions were already established. Researchers believe this site may have inspired the later construction of Stonehenge, offering crucial insights into Neolithic communities.

articles.title: Amid TMC, Shiv Sena (UBT) rebellions, the numbers that could benefit NDA in Parliament
articles.description: The NDA currently holds 293 seats in the 540-member Lok Sabha. A two-thirds majority would require support from around 360 members.

articles.title: Is The Worst Over For Dalal Street? Expert Warns Markets May Be Getting Too Complacent
articles.description: Market sentiment has improved significantly in recent weeks, with equities rebounding sharply and global concerns easing. Optimism surrounding geopolitical developments, stabilizing energy prices, and a strong recovery in major markets such as the US has boosted investor confidence. In India, benchmark indices have recovered much of their earlier losses, while pressure on the rupee and inflation concerns have moderated. According to Prateek Nigudkar, Senior Fund Manager, Shriram AMC, investors should welcome the improving outlook but remain cautious, as several risks continue to linger beneath the surface. While RBI measures have helped stabilize financial markets and support the currency, uncertainty around global growth and market valuations remains. Here's why experts believe the outlook has improved, but the markets may still not be completely out of the woods.

articles.title: The Accent Olympics: India’s favourite hobby is judging English
articles.description: From Mallu accents to American twangs, India's obsession with how English should sound reveals deeper anxieties about class, aspiration and belonging.

articles.title: Ukraine hits Moscow in 'large-scale' attack, sparking fires and evacuations
articles.description: Ukraine launched a massive drone assault on Moscow, causing fires and evacuations. The attack targeted the capital and its surrounding regions. Russian air defenses reported intercepting hundreds of drones. This significant strike occurred as President Putin prepared to host Southeast Asian leaders in Kazan. The conflict in Ukraine continues with no diplomatic resolution in sight.

articles.description: Construction work of a dam in Yarlung Tsangpo River has picked up pace in recent months

articles.title: Amid reports of internal split, Uddhav Thackeray keeps a close watch on 20 MLAs
articles.description: In an apparent bid to assess loyalties and prevent further uncertainty, Shiv Sena (UBT) issued a whip directing all its Lok Sabha MPs to attend a parliamentary party meeting in Delhi later today.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh to tap private sector to plug rural healthcare gaps
articles.description: The five-year pilot will see private operators run the day-to-day operations at 18 community health centres. The government will retain ownership of buildings and provide medicines and basic infrastructure. Private partners will be responsible for staffing, including specialist doctors, and service delivery

articles.title: NSE IPO OFS: Who's Selling Shares, Who Isn't? DRHP Reveals Plans Of Its Biggest Investors
articles.description: The proposed 14.89 crore-share NSE IPO is entirely an offer for sale, with several institutional investors planning to pare holdings while others stay invested.

articles.title: ‘People thought I was crazy’: Former Delhi Police officer quits government job and starts over in Australia
articles.description: A former Delhi Police Sub-Inspector, Nishant Tomar, has garnered attention for leaving his secure government job in India to relocate to Australia. He successfully built a new life from scratch, becoming a homeowner and a real estate agent in Adelaide, emphasizing that his journey was driven by taking chances and perseverance, not luck.

articles.title: 'Mail received from Messi's office': Dilip Ghosh says event row brought 'defame to Bengal'
articles.description: West Bengal minister Dilip Ghosh says a mail from Lionel Messi's office stated the football legend was not involved in the controversial Kolkata event, as the probe into alleged irregularities continues.

articles.title: Noor Singla Tops ICAI CA Final May 2026 Exam With 83.17% Marks
articles.description: ICAI CA Final May 2026 Topper: Noor Singla from Patiala secured AIR 1 in the CA Final May 2026 exam with 499 out of 600 marks, scoring 83.17 percent.
